title: Project analysis setup overview | SonarQube Server Documentation
content:
# Project analysis setup overview

## [Prerequisites and recommendations](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/overview/#prerequisites "Prerequisites and recommendations")

SonarQube Server is integrated with your DevOps platform / CI tool

It is highly recommended that you integrate SonarQube Server with your DevOps platform or CI tool. See:

- [GitHub](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/github-integration/setting-up-at-global-level/introduction/ "GitHub")
- [GitLab](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/gitlab-integration/global-setup/ "GitLab")
- [Bitbucket Server or Data Center](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/bitbucket-integration/bitbucket-server-integration/ "Bitbucket Server or Data Center")
- [Bitbucket Cloud](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/bitbucket-integration/bitbucket-cloud-integration/ "Bitbucket Cloud")
- [Azure DevOps](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/azure-devops-integration/introduction/ "Azure DevOps")
- [Jenkins](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/ci-integration/jenkins-integration/global-setup/ "Jenkins")

The SonarScanner is installed on the CI/CD host

The [SonarScanner](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/analysis-overview/ "SonarScanner") must be installed on the CI/CD host. You must install the scanner that is most appropriate for your needs depending on your build system: Gradle, Maven, .NET, NPM, or Python. For other project types, use the SonarScanner CLI which requires more manual configuration.

For installation requirements, see [General requirements on scanner environment](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/scanner-environment/general-requirements/ "General requirements on scanner environment").

For installation instructions, see the corresponding SonarScanner section: [Maven](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-maven/ "Maven"), [Gradle](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-gradle/ "Gradle"), [NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/dotnet/installing/ "NET"), [NPM](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/npm/installing/ "NPM"), [Python](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-python/ "Python"), or [CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner/ "CLI").

[SCM metadata](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scm-integration/ "SCM metadata") used during the analysis will be automatically imported from Git and SVN. Other providers require additional plugins.

## [Creating your SonarQube Server project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/overview/#creating-sq-project "Creating your SonarQube Server project")

Your project repository is represented in SonarQube Server by a project.

You can create a SonarQube Server project in the SonarQube Server UI before starting the first project analysis. Or you can start your first project analysis to automatically create the SonarQube Server project on the server (SonarQube Server creates automatically a new project if the received project key does not exist in its database).

To create a project, see [Creating and importing projects](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/project-administration/creating-and-importing-projects/ "Creating and importing projects").

## [Integrating the SonarQube Server analysis into your CI or build pipeline](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/overview/#integrating-into-ci-or-build "Integrating the SonarQube Server analysis into your CI or build pipeline")

To integrate the SonarQube Server analysis into your CI pipeline, see the corresponding section:

- Jenkins: [Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to a Jenkins job](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/ci-integration/jenkins-integration/add-analysis-to-job/ "Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to a Jenkins job")
- GitHub Actions: [Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your GitHub Actions workflow](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/github-integration/adding-analysis-to-github-actions-workflow/ "Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your GitHub Actions workflow")
- GitLab CI/CD:  [Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your GitLab CI/CD pipeline](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/gitlab-integration/adding-analysis-to-gitlab-ci-cd/ "Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your GitLab CI/CD pipeline")
- Azure Pipelines: [Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your Azure build pipeline](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/azure-devops-integration/adding-analysis-to-pipeline/introduction/ "Adding the SonarQube Server analysis to your Azure build pipeline")
- [Bitbucket Pipelines](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/devops-platform-integration/bitbucket-integration/bitbucket-cloud-integration/ "Bitbucket Pipelines")

To integrate the SonarQube Server analysis into your build pipeline, see the scanner invoking instructions in the corresponding section:  [Maven](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-maven/ "Maven"), [Gradle](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-gradle/ "Gradle"), [NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/dotnet/using/ "NET"), [NPM](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/npm/using/ "NPM"), [Python](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-python/ "Python"), or [CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner/ "CLI").

For general information about SonarQube Server integration with a CI or build pipeline, see [SonarQube Server analysis overview](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/analysis-overview/ "SonarQube Server analysis overview").

## [Adjusting the analysis of your project](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/overview/#adjusting-analysis "Adjusting the analysis of your project")

The analysis performed by the SonarScanner is configured through analysis parameters. The following applies:

- A few analysis parameters are mandatory.
- Many analysis parameters, such as those defining the analysis scope, have a default value and can be adjusted.
- Analysis parameters allow you to include the code and test coverage in your analysis, or to import issues generated by a third-party analyzer, etc.

SonarQube Server manages the analysis parameters through sonar properties (The sonar property key has the following syntax: `sonar.<property>`.).

You can configure the analysis parameters in different places. For more information, see [Analysis parameters](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/analysis-parameters/ "Analysis parameters") and the respective SonarScanner section: [Maven](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-maven/ "Maven"), [Gradle](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-gradle/ "Gradle"), [NET](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/dotnet/using/ "NET"), [NPM](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/npm/configuring/ "NPM"), [Python](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner-for-python/ "Python"), or [CLI](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/scanners/sonarscanner/ "CLI").

You can:

- Include the code and test coverage in your analysis: see [Test coverage](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/test-coverage/overview/ "Test coverage").
- Adjust the analysis scope: see [Analysis scope](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/project-administration/setting-analysis-scope/introduction/ "Analysis scope").
- Import issues generated by third-party analyzers: see the [Importing external issues](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-server/latest/analyzing-source-code/importing-external-issues/external-analyzer-reports/ "Importing external issues") section.
